methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axibo AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ishaan Mehta that has raised C$12M to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers across the robotics and manufacturing sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private entities including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable in this case. Despite identifying 11 same-sector peers, none meet the filtering criteria for meaningful triangulation—specifically, no peers exist at the same seed stage, adjacent stages, or similar valuation tiers. The dataset lacks the minimum three comparable datapoints required for robust peer-median analysis. A broader all-sector median approach would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of robotics subsegments and business models.

Without a sufficiently narrow peer set, traditional comparable-company analysis cannot be reliably applied. A more defensible valuation would require either: (i) expanding the dataset to capture additional seed-stage robotics companies with disclosed valuations; (ii) employing alternative methods such as venture capital method or cost-to-develop analysis; or (iii) conducting deeper diligence into the five identified private peers to assess whether stage and valuation-tier proxies can be established post-hoc. The current data supports only a holding position pending further information rather than a specific valuation range.
